title: Reading and Use of English - Part 6
description: You are going to read an article from which six sentences have been removed. Choose from the sentences A–G the one which fits each gap (37–42). There is one extra sentence which you do not need to use.

content.title: The Sweet Side of the City: Urban Beekeeping
content.text: On rooftops across London, Paris and New York, a quiet revolution is taking place. Thousands of honeybee colonies now live among chimneys, solar panels and water towers, tended by a growing community of urban beekeepers. (37) [37] La afición comenzó modestamente pero se ha extendido con rapidez. [/37] What began as a niche hobby has grown into a serious movement, with cities reporting record numbers of new hive registrations every year. || The bees themselves seem to thrive in urban environments. Surprisingly, city honey is often richer and more complex in flavour than its rural counterpart. (38) [38] La variedad de plantas disponibles contribuye a un sabor más elaborado. [/38] Parks, gardens, window boxes and street trees provide a remarkably diverse buffet of nectar sources throughout the season. Rural bees, by contrast, often rely on vast monocultures of a single crop, which limits the depth of their honey's taste. || Urban beekeeping does present unique challenges, however. Space is always at a premium, and neighbours do not always welcome the idea of thousands of stinging insects next door. (39) [39] La formación adecuada es fundamental para gestionar estos problemas. [/39] Most cities now require beekeepers to register their hives and complete a basic training course before setting up. This not only keeps colonies healthy but also helps to prevent overcrowding, which can lead to swarming. || Beyond the honey itself, urban beekeepers are motivated by a deeper purpose. Bees are essential pollinators, and their presence in cities has measurable benefits for local biodiversity. (40) [40] Los estudios confirman un impacto positivo en la flora urbana. [/40] Research in Berlin found that urban green spaces with active hives contained significantly more plant species than those without. The ripple effect on insects, birds and small mammals is considerable. || Some municipalities have gone further, incorporating beekeeping into school curricula and community programmes. Children who tend hives develop patience, scientific curiosity and a respect for the natural world. (41) [41] El aprendizaje práctico conecta a los jóvenes con la naturaleza de forma directa. [/41] Teachers report that students who struggle in conventional classrooms often excel when given responsibility for a living colony. || Not everyone is convinced, though. Critics argue that an explosion of managed honeybees in cities could actually harm wild bee populations by competing for the same limited food resources. (42) [42] El debate entre expertos sigue abierto y sin una conclusión definitiva. [/42] For now, urban beekeepers press on, jar by jar, hive by hive, sweetening the city one rooftop at a time.

paragraphs.A: Proper training and clear regulations have helped address many of the concerns raised by residents.
paragraphs.B: This is largely because city bees have access to an extraordinary variety of flowering plants.
paragraphs.C: Several studies have confirmed that urban pollination leads to greater plant diversity in green spaces.
paragraphs.D: It is a debate that scientists and beekeeping associations have yet to fully resolve.
paragraphs.E: Hands-on responsibility for a living creature gives young people a connection to nature that textbooks cannot replicate.
paragraphs.F: In many cities, the number of registered hives has doubled in less than a decade.
paragraphs.G: Harvesting honey from a high-rise can be a logistical challenge, requiring specialised lightweight equipment.

questions.explanation: F proporciona datos concretos sobre el crecimiento rápido del movimiento, conectando con la idea de expansión mencionada antes.
B explica la causa del sabor más rico de la miel urbana gracias a la variedad de plantas, encajando perfectamente con el argumento siguiente.
A introduce la solución a los desafíos mencionados (vecinos, espacio), enlazando con los requisitos de registro y formación.
C anuncia los estudios que confirman el impacto positivo en la biodiversidad, preparando el ejemplo de Berlín.
E desarrolla el beneficio educativo del cuidado de colmenas, conectando con la observación de los profesores sobre los alumnos.
D resume el debate sin resolver entre expertos, cerrando el párrafo crítico antes del cierre optimista del artículo.
